message:
fix(migrate): use storage factory for backend-aware database access

The migrate command hardcoded sqlite.New() in several code paths, causing
failures when the backend is Dolt (directory-based storage). This fixes:

- handleUpdateRepoID: uses storagefactory.NewFromConfigWithOptions instead
  of sqlite.New, so bd migrate --update-repo-id works with both backends
- Main migrate flow: detects Dolt backend early and handles metadata
  updates via storage factory, avoiding the SQLite-only detectDatabases
  flow that cannot find Dolt directories
- handleInspect: opens database via storage factory for read-only inspection
- handleToSeparateBranch: opens database via storage factory
- doctor fix/repo_fingerprint: reinitialize option now detects backend and
  uses os.RemoveAll for Dolt directories vs os.Remove for SQLite files

Fixes: gt-dolt-metadata

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Executed-By: beads/polecats/jasper
Rig: beads
Role: polecats
